items.hook: Not the End of the World is Christopher Brookmyre's third novel, and the first not to feature Jack Parlabane, Christopher Brookmyre's most used character.
items.body: The novel is also the first book Brookmyre has written that is based solely outside of the United Kingdom. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: Randwick is located 6 kilometres south-east of the Sydney central business district and is the administrative centre for the local government area of the City of Randwick.
items.body: Randwick is a suburb in the Eastern Suburbs of Sydney, in the state of New South Wales, Australia. Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.

items.hook: The Wall: Growing Up Behind the Iron Curtain is a children's book written and illustrated by Peter Sís.
items.body: It received both the American Library Association's Caldecott Honor and ALA's 2008 Robert Silbert Medal for the most distinguished informational book for young readers. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: King Abdullah I of Jordan is assassinated by a Palestinian while attending Friday prayers in Jerusalem.
items.body: Abdullah I was the ruler of Jordan from 11 April 1921 until his assassination in 1951. Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.

items.hook: Hagitude author Sharon Blackie: ‘At 60 I wasn’t ready to give up, I was just starting’
items.body:
The writer of cult hit If Women Rose Rooted is on a mission to bring folklore to modern readers. She talks about confronting her fears, communing with nature – and the power that comes with age

Like many of the wise women in her bo

items.hook: Soyuz MS-13 is launched to the International Space Station on the 50th anniversary of the Apollo 11 moon landing.
items.body: Soyuz MS-13, also designated ISS flight 59S, was a crewed Soyuz mission launched on 20 July 2019 – the 50th anniversary of the first Moon landing – carrying three members of the Expedition 60 crew to the International Space Station: a Russian commander, an American flight engineer, and a European flight engineer. The world is stranger than it lets on.

items.hook: British Columbia joins the Canadian Confederation.
items.body: British Columbia is the westernmost province of Canada. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: Toronto unveils 2026 galas and special presentations
items.body: The Toronto festival sections take in 56 films from nearly 30 countries.

items.hook: Vamba Sherif is a Liberian-born writer residing in the Netherlands.
items.body: He writes in English and Dutch. Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.

items.hook: Sheares helped establish the National Museum Art Gallery in 1976, curating its inaugural exhibition and other major exhibitions.
items.body: Constance Alice Chengliu Sheares is a Singaporean arts administrator, curator, and writer. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.hook: In the Preußenschlag, German President Hindenburg places Prussia directly under the rule of the national government.
items.body: The 1932 Prussian coup d'état or Preußenschlag took place on 20 July 1932, when Reich President Paul von Hindenburg, at the request of Franz von Papen, then Reich Chancellor of Germany, replaced the legal government of the Free State of Prussia with von Papen as Reich Commissioner. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.hook: Ofcom launches investigation into Married at First Sight UK
items.body: In May, BBC Panorama revealed rape allegations from two women on the show, which the men involved deny.

items.hook: The Civil Marriage Act legalizes same-sex marriage in Canada.
items.body: The Civil Marriage Act is a federal statute legalizing same-sex marriage across Canada. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.hook: The Shanghai Spell is a 2002 film written and directed by Fernando Trueba.
items.body: The film is based on the 1993 novel of the same name by Juan Marsé. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: The Fate of Fenella was an experiment in consecutive novel writing inspired by J.
items.body: Wood and published in his magazine The Gentlewoman in twenty-four parts between 1891 and 1892. The world is stranger than it lets on.

items.hook: PostgreSQL features transactions with atomicity, consistency, isolation, durability (ACID) properties, automatically updatable views, materialized views, triggers, foreign keys, and stored procedures.
items.body: PostgreSQL, also known as Postgres, is a free and open-source relational database management system (RDBMS) emphasizing extensibility and SQL compliance. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: In Finland, a new electoral law is ratified, guaranteeing the country the first and equal right to vote in the world. Finnish women are the first in Europe to receive the right to vote.
items.body: Finland, officially the Republic of Finland, is a Nordic country in Northern Europe. Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.
